creator: Jagmeet Singh
title: (No AI) Google, Accel India accelerator chooses 5 startups and none are ‘AI wrappers’
dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
content: Google and Accel say about 70% of AI startup pitches tied to India were "wrappers" as they reviewed more than 4,000 applications for their Atoms cohort.

title: (No AI) This is not a fly uploaded to a computer
author: Robert Hart
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Screenshot-2026-03-13-at-17.18.37.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Last week, a few posts about a so-called virtual "embodied fly" tore through X, boosted by AI hype accounts and excited commenters who didn't seem to understand what it was they were excited about.

The videos came from San Francisco-based Eon Systems, which says it's working toward "digital human intelligence" and claims it wants to build a full digital emulation of a mouse brain within the next two years - a timeline that is, to put it generously, ambitious. Cofounder Alexander Wissner-Gross [shared](https://x.com/alexwg/status/2030217301929132323?s=20) the original clip publicly, calling it the "world's first embodiment of a whole-brain emulation that produces multiple behaviors" and hinting a …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/894587/fly-brain-computer-upload)

Last week, a few posts about a so-called virtual "embodied fly" tore through X, boosted by AI hype accounts and excited commenters who didn't seem to understand what it was they were excited about.
The videos came from San Francisco-based Eon Systems, which says it's working toward "digital human intelligence" and claims it wants to build a full digital emulation of a mouse brain within the next two years - a timeline that is, to put it generously, ambitious. Cofounder Alexander Wissner-Gross shared the original clip publicly, calling it the "world's first embodiment of a whole-brain emulation that produces multiple behaviors" and hinting a …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) OpenAI’s adult mode will reportedly be smutty, not pornographic
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![Illustration of lips in a browser window.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25313355/STK464_PORN_ADULT_CONTENT_2A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

So when exactly is smut considered porn? | Cath Virginia / The Verge | Photos from Getty Images

OpenAI's delayed "adult mode" for ChatGPT is expected to support saucy text conversations at launch, but not the chatbot's ability to generate images, voice, or video. Speaking to [_The Wall Street Journal_](https://www.wsj.com/tech/ai/openai-adult-mode-chatgpt-f9e5fc1a?mod=rss_Technology), an unnamed OpenAI spokesperson described content that will be provided by the upcoming feature as smut rather than pornography, allowing ChatGPT users to generate textual chats with adult themes.

The feature was initially announced in October, with [OpenAI CEO Sam Altman](https://x.com/sama/status/1978129344598827128) claiming that the company had [managed to mitigate](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/779053/sam-altman-says-chatgpt-will-stop-talking-about-suicide-with-teens) enough of the "serious mental health issues" with its AI model to relax safety restrictions and introduce "[erotica for ver …](https://www.theverge.com/news/799312/openai-chatgpt-erotica-sam-altman-verified-adults)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/895130/openai-chatgpt-adult-mode-text-smut-written-erotica)
contentSnippet:
So when exactly is smut considered porn? | Cath Virginia / The Verge | Photos from Getty Images	

OpenAI's delayed "adult mode" for ChatGPT is expected to support saucy text conversations at launch, but not the chatbot's ability to generate images, voice, or video. Speaking to The Wall Street Journal, an unnamed OpenAI spokesperson described content that will be provided by the upcoming feature as smut rather than pornography, allowing ChatGPT users to generate textual chats with adult themes.
The feature was initially announced in October, with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman claiming that the company had managed to mitigate enough of the "serious mental health issues" with its AI model to relax safety restrictions and introduce "erotica for ver …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Matt Burgess
title: (No AI) ‘100 Video Calls Per Day’: Models Are Applying to Be the Face of AI Scams
dc:creator: Matt Burgess
content: Dozens of Telegram channels reviewed by WIRED include job listings for “AI face models.” The (mostly) women who land these gigs are likely being used to dupe victims out of their money.
contentSnippet: Dozens of Telegram channels reviewed by WIRED include job listings for “AI face models.” The (mostly) women who land these gigs are likely being used to dupe victims out of their money.

creator: Reece Rogers
title: (No AI) Google's AI Search Results Love to Refer You Back to Google
dc:creator: Reece Rogers
content: The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.
contentSnippet: The company's generative AI search tools increasingly cite its own services, like Google Search and YouTube, over third-party publishers.
